skill.name: wordpress-plugin-development
skill.description: WordPress plugin development workflow covering plugin architecture, hooks, admin interfaces, REST API, and security best practices.
skill.summary: Guide WordPress plugin builds from setup through security review and testing.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: The static external command findings are false positives caused by Markdown inline code and fenced prompt examples. The weak cryptography findings are false positives from ordinary prose substrings such as description and design. No evidence found of executable code, prompt injection, network access, credential access, or malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Static External Command Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The reported backtick locations are Markdown skill names, fenced prompts, and a directory tree. They are not shell execution paths.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is Markdown documentation and copy-paste prompt text. No executable command syntax or runtime invocation exists in the skill file.

low_findings.title: Static Weak Cryptography Alerts Dismissed
low_findings.description: The weak cryptography alerts match normal documentation words and workflow names. No cryptographic API, hash function, or encryption implementation is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged lines contain descriptive workflow text, skill references, and a directory name. There is no code that selects or uses a weak algorithm.

content.user_title: Build WordPress Plugins with Structured Workflows
content.value_statement: WordPress plugin projects need consistent architecture, secure data handling, and clear release gates. This skill guides setup, hooks, admin screens, REST APIs, testing, and security review.
actual_capabilities: Breaks plugin development into setup, architecture, hooks, admin, database, REST API, security, and testing phases.
Lists related specialist skills to invoke for each phase.
Provides copy-paste prompts for scaffolding, architecture, security review, and testing tasks.
Defines a conventional WordPress plugin directory structure.
Includes quality gates for activation, hooks, admin behavior, security, tests, and documentation.
limitations: Does not include executable scripts or automated scaffolding.
Does not provide complete PHP plugin source code.
Depends on separate skills for detailed implementation guidance.
Requires human review for WordPress version and project-specific security needs.

use_cases.title: Plan a New Plugin
use_cases.description: Create a phase-based build plan before writing plugin files.

use_cases.title: Add Admin and API Features
use_cases.description: Organize admin pages, settings registration, REST routes, permissions, and validation work.

use_cases.title: Prepare for Release Review
use_cases.description: Check activation, hooks, tests, documentation, and security controls before publishing.

prompt_templates.title: Start Plugin Setup
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner setup for a new plugin.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan a new WordPress plugin. Include files, activation hooks, autoloading, text domain, and first quality gates.

prompt_templates.title: Design Plugin Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to design a WordPress plugin architecture with classes, loader responsibilities, dependency boundaries, and lifecycle handling.

prompt_templates.title: Build Admin and REST Features
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to plan admin settings pages and REST API endpoints. Include capability checks, validation, sanitization, and documentation tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Run Pre-Release Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this workflow to review a WordPress plugin before release. Cover security controls, database queries, tests, activation behavior, and documentation gaps.

output_examples.input: Plan a plugin that stores custom customer preferences.
output: A phased build checklist covering setup, architecture, database design, admin settings, REST endpoints, security review, and tests.
Suggested specialist skill prompts for schema design, admin interface planning, and security auditing.
A quality gate report covering activation, hook behavior, permissions, sanitization, escaping, test coverage, and documentation.
A prioritized list of release blockers and follow-up tasks.

best_practices: Use nonces, capability checks, input sanitization, and output escaping for every user-facing path.
Plan plugin lifecycle, upgrade routines, and deactivation behavior before adding features.
Pair this workflow with project-specific PHP tests and WordPress compatibility checks.
anti_patterns: Skipping permission callbacks for REST API routes.
Mixing admin, public, database, and activation logic in one large file.
Publishing without testing activation, hooks, security controls, and documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill generate a complete WordPress plugin?
faq.answer: No. It provides a structured workflow and prompts for planning, implementation, security review, and testing.

faq.question: Can this skill help with REST API endpoints?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes a REST API phase covering routes, callbacks, permissions, validation, and documentation.

faq.question: Does this skill include security guidance?
faq.answer: Yes. It calls out nonces, capability checks, sanitization, escaping, secure queries, and security auditing.

faq.question: Is this skill specific to WooCommerce plugins?
faq.answer: No. It is a general WordPress plugin workflow and links WooCommerce as a related workflow.

faq.question: What tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.

faq.question: Does this skill run commands on my machine?
faq.answer: No. The reviewed file is documentation with prompts and checklists, not executable automation.
